import tensorflow as tf

# Load the original model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define quantization parameters
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Define a representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Get sample input data as a numpy array in a method of your choosing.
        input_data = tf.random.normal([1, 224, 224, 3], dtype=tf.float32)
        yield [input_data]

# Convert the model to TFLite with quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert and save the quantized model
quantized_tflite_model = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(quantized_tflite_model)

import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Convert the model to a TFLite model with quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Define a representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate random input data within the expected range
        yield [np.random.uniform(-1, 1, size=(1, *original_model.input_shape[1:])).astype(np.float32)]

converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, the converter throws an error
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Convert and save the model to TFLite format
tflite_quant_model = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_quant_model)

```python
import tensorflow as tf

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Convert the model to a TFLite model with quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type
converter.representative_dataset = lambda: tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices(
    (np.random.uniform(-1, 1, size=(1000, *original_model.input_shape[1:])),)
).batch(1)

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, the converter throws an error
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Convert and save the model to TFLite format
tflite_quant_model = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_quant_model)
```

original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Convert the model to a TFLite model with quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type
converter.representative_dataset = lambda: tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices(
    (np.random.uniform(-1, 1, size=(1000, *original_model.input_shape[1:])),)
).batch(1)

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, the converter throws an error
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Convert and save the model to TFLite format
tflite_quant_model = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_quant_model)
```

inference_output_type = tf.uint8

inference_output_type = tf.uint8

import tensorflow as tf

model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Convert the model to TFLite with post-training quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]

converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert and save the model
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()

import tensorflow as tf

# Load the original model
model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Convert the model to TFLite with post-training quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Define a generator function for calibration data
def representative_data_gen():
    # Replace "path_to_calibration_dataset" with the actual path to your calibration dataset
    # Example: Assuming you have a numpy array of images named `calibration_images`
    import numpy as np
    calibration_images = np.random.rand(100, 28, 28, 3).astype(np.float32)  # Example shape and type
    for input_value in calibration_images:
        yield [input_value]

# Set the representative dataset for quantization
converter.representative_dataset = representative_data_gen

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, the converter throws an error
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert and save the model
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_model_quant)

import tensorflow as tf

# Define the quantization parameters
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define a representative dataset generator function for calibration
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate random input data that is similar to your training data
        yield [tf.random.uniform(shape=(1, 224, 224, 3), minval=0.0, maxval=1.0, dtype=tf.float32)]

# Convert the model to TFLite with quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

```python
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Define a representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    input_shape = (1, 224, 224, 3)  # Example shape, adjust as necessary
    for _ in range(100):
        yield [np.array(np.random.random_sample(input_shape), dtype=np.float32)]

converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert and save the TFLite model
quantized_tflite_model = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(quantized_tflite_model)
```

import tensorflow as tf

# Load the original model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Define representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate a random input data point within the expected range
        yield [tf.random.uniform(shape=(1, 224, 224, 3), minval=0.0, maxval=1.0, dtype=tf.float32)]

# Configure converter for quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert the model to TFLite format with quantization
quantized_tflite_model = converter.convert()

# Save the converted model to a file
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(quantized_tflite_model)

inference_output_type = tf.uint8

```python
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]

# Define the inference input and output types
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8

# Set the representative dataset for quantization
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Get sample input data as a numpy array in a method of your choosing
        yield [np.array(np.random.rand(1, 32, 32, 3), dtype=np.float32)]

converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, the converter throws an error
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type  # or tf.uint8 if you want to further quantize the input
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert and save the model to the specified path
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_model_quant)
```

original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Define the converter
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, the converter throws an error
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]

# Define a generator function that provides representative dataset examples
def representative_data_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Get sample input data as a numpy array in a method of your choosing.
        yield [np.array(np.random.random_sample((1, 224, 224, 3)), dtype=np.float32)]

# Set the representative dataset for quantization
converter.representative_dataset = representative_data_gen

# Set the input and output tensors to int8 (optional)
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type  # or tf.float32
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type  # or tf.uint8

# Convert the model
quantized_tflite_model = converter.convert()

# Save the quantized model to disk
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(quantized_tflite_model)

import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np
import random

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Create a converter object from the Keras model
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)

# Set the optimization flag to optimize for size and enable quantization
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Define a generator function that provides representative dataset examples
def representative_data_gen():
    # Load or generate a small sample of input data in float32 format
    # This should be a generator that yields samples from your validation set
    for _ in range(100):
        random_sample = np.random.rand(1, 64, 64, 3)  # Example shape, adjust as necessary
        yield [np.array(random_sample, dtype=np.float32)]

# Set the representative dataset for quantization
converter.representative_dataset = representative_data_gen

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, they are still converted anyway
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]

# Set the input and output tensors to int8 (except the inputs)
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert the model
quantized_tflite_model = converter.convert()

# Save the quantized TFLite model to a file
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(quantized_tflite_model)

```python
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np
import random

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Create a converter object from the Keras model
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)

# Set the optimization flag to optimize for size and enable quantization
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops

# Define a generator function that provides representative dataset examples
def representative_data_gen():
    # Load or generate a small sample of input data in float32 format
    # This should be a generator that yields samples from your validation set
    for _ in range(100):
        random_sample = np.random.rand(1, 64, 64, 3)  # Example shape, adjust as necessary
        yield [np.array(random_sample, dtype=np.float32)]

# Set the representative dataset for quantization
converter.representative_dataset = representative_data_gen

# Ensure that if any ops can't be quantized, they are still converted anyway
converter.target_spec.supported_types = [tf.int8]

# Set the input and output tensors to int8 (except the inputs)
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert the model
quantized_tflite_model = converter.convert()

# Save the quantized TFLite model to a file
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(quantized_tflite_model)
```

import tensorflow as tf

# Define the conversion parameters based on the data type configuration
input_datatype = "float32"
output_datatype = "uint8"
original_model_path = "models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras"
converted_model_path = "models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite"

# Load the original Keras model
model = tf.keras.models.load_model(original_model_path)

# Define representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate a representative input sample. The actual implementation may vary based on your data.
        yield [tf.random.uniform(shape=(1, 224, 224, 3), minval=0, maxval=255, dtype=tf.float32)]

# Convert the model to TFLite with 8-bit integer quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]
converter.inference_input_type = tf.int8  # or tf.float32 if you don't want to quantize the input
converter.inference_output_type = tf.uint8

# Convert and save the model
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()
with open(converted_model_path, 'wb') as f:
    f.write(tflite_model_quant)

### OBJECTIVE ###

    - output_datatype= "uint8"

```python
import tensorflow as tf

# Define the conversion parameters based on the data type configuration
input_datatype = "float32"
output_datatype = "uint8"
original_model_path = "models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras"
converted_model_path = "models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite"

# Load the original Keras model
model = tf.keras.models.load_model(original_model_path)

# Define representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate a representative input sample. The actual implementation may vary based on your data.
        yield [tf.random.uniform(shape=(1, 224, 224, 3), minval=0, maxval=255, dtype=tf.float32)]

# Convert the model to TFLite with 8-bit integer quantization
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]
converter.inference_input_type = tf.int8  # or tf.float32 if you don't want to quantize the input
converter.inference_output_type = tf.uint8

# Convert and save the model
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()
with open(converted_model_path, 'wb') as f:
    f.write(tflite_model_quant)
```

import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Define representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    input_shape = original_model.input_shape[1:]  # Get the shape of the model's input
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate a random input data of the same shape as your model's input
        yield [np.array(np.random.random_sample(input_shape), dtype=np.float32)]

# Set the optimization flag to optimize for size and specify the supported ops
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert the model to TFLite format with 8-bit integer quantization
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()

# Save the converted model to a file
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_model_quant)

import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

# Load the original Keras model
original_model = tf.keras.models.load_model("models/fruit_to_emoji/og_model/model.keras")

# Define conversion parameters for 8-bit integer quantization
inference_input_type = tf.float32
inference_output_type = tf.uint8
supported_ops = [tf.lite.OpsSet.TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8]

# Define representative dataset generator function
def representative_dataset_gen():
    input_shape = original_model.input_shape[1:]  # Get the shape of the model's input
    for _ in range(100):
        # Generate a random input data of the same shape as your model's input
        yield [np.array(np.random.random_sample(input_shape), dtype=np.float32)]

# Set the optimization flag to optimize for size and specify the supported ops
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(original_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
converter.representative_dataset = representative_dataset_gen
converter.target_spec.supported_ops = supported_ops
converter.inference_input_type = inference_input_type
converter.inference_output_type = inference_output_type

# Convert the model to TFLite format with 8-bit integer quantization
tflite_model_quant = converter.convert()

# Save the converted model to a file
with open("models/fruit_to_emoji/tflite_model/model_quant.tflite", "wb") as f:
    f.write(tflite_model_quant)
